title: Static Accessibility Evidence, WCAG Mapping, and Review Handoff · Execution Trace: Conformance Boundary
summary: The crawler counts alt values and headings then declares WCAG compliance without computed names, interactions, contrast, reflow, or disabled-user review. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go conformance boundary increment in WCAG-linked static finding ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Link findings to WCAG 2.2 criteria, affected users, page state, severity rationale, evidence, uncertainty, and human owner without conformance claims.
Flag keyboard, focus, motion, timing, status, target, contrast, and responsive risks and route them to controlled interaction review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Conformance Boundary · Theory Conformance Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 9141 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to map static image, structure, semantics, interaction risks, uncertainty, and WCAG review boundaries; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Build Conformance Boundary evidence · Theory Conformance Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-conformance-boundary-poi6qx" that uses case 7206 to map static image, structure, semantics, interaction risks, uncertainty, and WCAG review boundaries. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Link findings to WCAG 2.2 criteria, affected users, page state, severity rationale, evidence, uncertainty, and human owner without conformance claims.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Conformance Boundary model · Theory Conformance Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Zero static findings means the full site conforms to WCAG.
